channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Republicans try to weaken 50-year-old law protecting whales, seals and polar bears
item.description: BOOTHBAY HARBOR, Maine (AP) — Republican lawmakers are targeting one of the U.S.'s longest standing pieces of environmental legislation, credited with helping save rare whales from extinction.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Greece's famed Parthenon free of scaffolding for first time in decades
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — Walking along the broad pedestrian street that runs along the base of Athens’ famed Acropolis Hill, visitors can now enjoy something not seen in decades: an unobstructed, scaffolding-free view of the Parthenon temple. The scaffolding set up along the 5th century B.C.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Power restored to 800,000 in Kyiv after major Russian strikes on Ukraine's energy grid
item.description: Power was restored to over 800,000 residents in Kyiv on Saturday, a day after Russia launched major attacks on the Ukrainian power grid that caused blackouts across much of the country, and European leaders agreed to proceed toward using hundreds of billions of frozen Russian assets to support Ukrai
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Officials investigate blast at Tennessee explosives plant that left 18 missing and feared dead
item.description: Officials are investigating a blast that leveled an explosives plant in rural Tennessee, as families of the 18 people missing and feared dead wait anxiously for answers.
item.dc:creator: Travis Loller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A look at how Venezuelans in the US are reacting to Maria Corina Machado's Nobel Prize win
item.description: DORAL, Fla. (AP) — Venezuelans in “Little Venezuela” — the largest home for the country's natives in the United States — are welcoming the news that opposition leader Maria Corina Machado won the Nobel Peace Prize with bittersweetness as deportation threats loom.
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's quest for the Nobel Peace Prize falls short again despite high-profile nominations
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump was passed over for the Nobel Peace Prize on Friday despite jockeying from his fellow Republicans, various world leaders and — most vocally — himself.
item.dc:creator: MIKE PESOLI and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: WIC food program receives $300M to keep running during government shutdown
item.description: The Trump administration has provided a $300 million infusion to a food aid program for low-income mothers and children, easing concerns about funding during the government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Moriah Balingit, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: WGN employee detained amid ICE activity in Chicago, later released
item.description: An employee of WGN-TV was detained by two Border Patrol officers and later released after federal agents swarmed a busy intersection in Chicago Friday Morning.
item.dc:creator: Christine Flores, WFLA, NBC

item.title: WGN employee detained amid ICE activity on Chicago's North Side, later released
item.description: Witnesses who captured the incident on video spoke to WGN about the moments leading up to the staffer's detainment.
item.dc:creator: Christine Flores, NewsNation

item.title: Trump threatens tech export limits, new 100% tariff on Chinese imports starting Nov. 1 or sooner
item.description: Trump said that “starting November 1st, 2025 (or sooner, depending on any further actions or changes taken by China), the United States of America will impose a Tariff of 100% on China, over and above any Tariff that they are currently paying.
item.dc:creator: KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ground stop issued at busy Atlanta airport following fire incident with 'strong gas fumes,' FAA confirms
item.description: Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport experienced a ground stop Friday afternoon due to a fire incident involving "gas fumes," according to the FAA.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Missing mom found alive in forest as officials investigate ‘suspicious’ house fire
item.description: Britney Gard, 46, called 911 from a forest near Bainbridge, Indiana, ending a week-long search that began after her house caught fire under suspicious circumstances.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House to begin 'substantial' shutdown layoffs of federal workers
item.description: The White House said Friday it had started laying off federal workers, following through on a threat to do so amid an ongoing government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Arctic seals, more than half of bird species on latest threatened list
item.description: While many animals are increasingly at risk of disappearing forever, the updated list shows how species can come back from the brink with dedicated effort.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Sister Jean Dolores Schmidt, beloved chaplain at Loyola University Chicago, dies at 106
item.description: Sister Jean Dolores Schmidt, a beloved member of the Loyola University Chicago community who was well-known as the chaplain to the Loyola University men's basketball team, died Thursday at the age of 106, according to the university.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, Fox 5

item.title: Tesla self-driving software under investigation after traffic violations
item.description: The NHTSA pointed to 18 complaints and one media report in which Tesla vehicles using the self-driving software failed to remain stopped or to stop fully at a red light or failed to accurately detect the traffic signal.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, Fox 5

item.title: Remains discovered in backyard container are of a young girl: Police
item.description: NEW BRITAIN, Conn. (WTNH) — New Britain police Friday confirmed the human remains found in a large container in the backyard of an abandoned home on Wednesday are that of "an adolescent female.
item.dc:creator: Bailey Wright, NewsNation

item.title: 22 states are in a recession or on the brink: Moody’s
item.description: It’s more sobering news for the economy as last week U.S. hiring plans fell to their lowest level since 2009.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Nieves, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Most states planning to withdraw National Guard troops from DC this fall
item.description: Nearly half the roughly 2,450 National Guard troops deployed to Washington, D.C., amid President Donald Trump's crackdown on crime are reportedly set to leave the city later this fall.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: Gradeschoolers leave Las Vegas school campus without teachers noticing
item.description: "There was no footage, and nobody noticed that they were gone. My whole thing is how is a kid able to wonder off, and then the teacher doesn't notice that there's an empty seat in the class," Christian Peña said.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Peguero, NewsNation

item.title: Troops will miss paychecks next week without action on the government shutdown
item.description: The nation’s third shutdown in 12 years is once again raising anxiety levels among service members and their families because those in uniform are working without pay.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Firings of federal workers begin as White House seeks to pressure Democrats in government shutdown
item.description: The White House budget office said Friday that mass firings of federal workers have started in an attempt to exert more pressure on Democratic lawmakers as the government shutdown continues.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Family of man who died on Stardust Racers demands its closure after new reports of malfunction
item.description: The family of Kevin Rodriguez Zavala, the Epic Universe park visitor who died after riding the Stardust Racers roller coaster, issued a statement demanding that the ride be closed after new reports of restraint malfunctions.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth says US will host Qatari air force facility in Idaho
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Friday the U.S. will host a new Qatari air force facility in Idaho, where pilots will train to fly F-15s.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4

item.title: The Latest: Trump says he looking at 'massive increase' in tariffs against China
item.description: President Donald Trump says “there seems to be no reason” to meet with Chinese leader Xi Jinping as part of an upcoming trip to South Korea after China restricted exports of rare earths needed for American industry.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: CDC panel to review childhood vaccine schedules
item.description: The latest workgroup will consider "new vaccine technologies and ingredients, and the rise of vaccine hesitancy.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, NewsNation

item.title: DHS blames Democrats for shutdown in video to be shown at TSA checkpoints
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) is rolling out a new video of DHS Secretary Kristi Noem blaming Democrats for the government shutdown,
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: GOP senators urge RFK Jr. to crack down on medication abortion
item.description: Almost every Senate Republican on Thursday urged the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to “reevaluate” whether a newly approved generic version of the abortion drug mifepristone should stay on the market. The letter to Health and Human Services (HHS) Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Texas appeals court again pauses execution of Robert Roberson in shaken baby case
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Texas’ top criminal court on Thursday again paused the execution of Robert Roberson, just days before he was set to become the first person in the U.S. put to death for a murder conviction tied to a diagnosis of shaken baby syndrome.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Arizona sheriff's office misused millions meant to remedy racial profiling, report reveals
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — The sheriff’s office for metro Phoenix spent millions of dollars budgeted for compliance costs in a racial profiling case over Joe Arpaio’s immigration crackdowns on things that had little or nothing to do with a court-ordered overhaul of the agency, according to an expert’s report.
item.dc:creator: JACQUES BILLEAUD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump was nominated for the Nobel Peace Prize before. Experts say he's unlikely to win on Friday
item.description: Trump’s bid to win the Nobel Peace Prize has drawn added attention to the annual guessing game over who its next laureate will be.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Flight delayed or canceled during government shutdown? Here's what you should do
item.description: The federal government shutdown has entered its second week, and already shortages of air traffic controllers have strained operations and disrupted flights at some U.S. airports.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK and RIO YAMAT AP Transportation and Airlines Writers, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump announces first phase of Israel-Hamas ceasefire in Gaza
item.description: President Trump announced Wednesday a ceasefire has been reached between Israel and Hamas, paving the way for the release of 20 living hostages and relief for nearly 2 million Palestinians in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: Wyoming library director fired amid book dispute wins $700,000 settlement
item.description: FORT COLLINS, Colo. (AP) — A former Wyoming library director who was fired amid an uproar over books with sexual content and LGBTQ+ themes that some people complained were inappropriate for youngsters and who sought their removal from youth shelves will be paid $700,000 after settling a lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Mead Gruver, KRON4

item.title: Florida Republican unveils bill threatening state funding if colleges don't rename roads after Charlie Kirk
item.description: Florida state Rep. Kevin Steele (R) filed a bill Tuesday that threatens state funding to public universities and colleges if they do not rename specific roads after slain conservative activist Charlie Kirk.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, ABC 4

item.title: Indiana man convicted in 2001 rape and murder of teenager to be executed by lethal injection
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — An Indiana man convicted in the 2001 rape and murder of a teenage girl was set to die by lethal injection early Friday in the state’s third execution since resuming capital punishment last year.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: GOP senators increasingly anxious about Trump’s aggressive use of National Guard
item.description: Republican senators are increasingly uneasy about President Trump’s standoff with Democratic governors over deploying National Guard troops from other states to Portland, Ore., and Chicago.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, ABC 4
